author: Aikat, A. C., M.A.
title: Notes on P. S. Allen's Selections from the writings of James Anthony Froude
notes: Section headed '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. ENGLISH—LANGUAGE serials continue the 1910 annual sequence (9 ff.). Exam-cram apparatus on Froude selections.

author: Knowlton, H. T.
title: Oral Lessons in English, illustrating the direct method of teaching foreign languages, reprinted from the Punjab Educational Journal, 1902
notes: Same Knowlton whose School Management was translated into Punjabi and Urdu in Q3. No price printed.
marks: cursive pencil scribble left margin (spans serials 9–10)

flags.issue: no price line printed for this entry

author: Morrison, H. W.
title: An Analysis of the second edition of Carter's History of English Legal Institutions
notes: 50 copies — smallest run category; law-exam study aid. Reg 1393 is the highest number seen in the 1910 sequence.

flags.issue: no price line visible for this entry

author: Jiwan Ram, Pandit
title: The Krishna Perpetual Calendar
copyright: Pandit Jiwan Ram, Lahore; No. 187, dated the 12th December, 1910.

title: Report of the First Punjab Hindu Conference held at Lahore on the 21st and 22nd of October, 1909
notes: Proceedings of the first Punjab Hindu Conference (Hindu Sabha movement's founding event) — reg 1165, first registration of the quarter.

author: Nihal Singh, Raizada
title: Geometry Made Easy
publisher: The author who is a teacher in the Municipal Board School, Chiniot

author: Muhammad Hasan, Hafiz
title: Sabil-un-Najat
gloss: A collection of forty traditions enjoining the veneration of sacred relics, etc.
notes: Published from Srinagar (Kashmir), printed Amritsar — likely tied to the Prophet's relic shrine (Hazratbal) devotion.

author: Jamita Ram, B.
title: Sangit Rup Basant
gloss: Story noticed in entry No. 2 at page 10 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1909.
